ranked_movies.title: The Lobster
ranked_movies.reason: Highly discussed as a uniquely bizarre, hilarious, and horrifying dystopian film that profoundly impacts viewers and sparks deep conversation about love and society.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a profoundly traumatic yet artistically intentional film that severely impacts viewers, making it a benchmark for emotionally devastating cinema.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a mind-blowing, unsettling documentary that challenges perspectives on history, morality, and filmmaking itself.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently mentioned as a shocking and impactful documentary that reveals an unbelievable true story, leaving viewers astounded.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a prime example of modern 'elevated horror' that uses terror to explore deep themes of grief and family trauma.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its intentionally nauseating and traumatic impact, using technical filmmaking to create a deeply unsettling experience.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a chilling 'comedy' that explores dark, intersecting storylines and leaves a lasting, disturbing impression.

ranked_movies.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently suggested as the next logical step for fans of 'The Lobster', known for its bizarre tone and ability to provoke strong emotional reactions.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a brutally honest and shocking film about teenage life, with an ending that is 'burned in the memory' of viewers.

ranked_movies.title: Logan
ranked_movies.reason: Praised as an unexpected masterpiece within the superhero genre, celebrated for its brutal action, character development, and compelling story.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a surprise fantastic comedy with a perfect cast, exceeding low expectations to become a beloved cult classic.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a jaw-dropping true crime documentary series that unfolds with shocking real-life revelations.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as an extremely dark film that shakes viewers to their core with its themes and powerful performances.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a beautifully written film focused on real human moments, connection, and awkwardness, feeling like 'poetry in motion'.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Analyzed in-depth for its critique of masculinity, creative obsession, and familial neglect, solidifying its status as a film that sparks endless discussion.

ranked_movies.title: The Emperor's New Groove
ranked_movies.reason: Unexpectedly praised as one of Disney's best, known for its originality and humor despite a troubled production.

ranked_movies.title: Talk to Me
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a modern example of art horror that serves as a vehicle for underlying messages about trauma and society.

ranked_movies.title: Paradise Lost
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a documentary that is both shocking and infuriating, detailing a tragic real-world miscarriage of justice.

ranked_movies.title: The Florida Project
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a powerful drama that explores poverty and addiction with humanity and dignity, often fucking viewers up emotionally.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for its iconic blend of horror and comedy, particularly the memorable line 'We're leaving' following scenes of unspeakable terror.
